attacking_style: Launches aggressive attacks inside 1km on uphill finishes, often going early enough to gain a gap but mistiming the final sprint. Shows willingness to attack from breakaways on technical finales.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with limited team support, relying on personal timing rather than leadout trains. Exploits when sprinters' teams lose control on gradient changes.
terrain_preference: Steep uphill finishes and technical finales where pure sprinters struggle. Thrives in reduced bunch scenarios where gradient disrupts organized leadouts.
vulnerability: Timing judgment on uphill attacks—goes too early inside final kilometer, allowing others to close gap and outpace in final meters. Third places suggest strong but not strongest kick.
key_patterns: Attacks prematurely inside final 1km on uphill finishes, gaining gaps but fading to third (2 observations)
Performs well in technical/challenging finales requiring positioning skill over pure power (2 observations)
Operates from breakaways or reduced groups rather than mass bunch sprints (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: showed consistent ability to contest uphill/technical finishes but repeated third places indicate plateau rather than progression
